title: Dasistoma pedicularia
taxonomy: Dasistoma pedicularia is classified as a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales, which includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers. The plant's classification also places it in the family Scrophulariaceae, which is part of the clade Asterids. Further classification also includes the subfamily Scrophularioideae. The plant's complete classification is Dasistoma pedicularia, family Scrophulariaceae, order Lamiales.

intro: Dasistoma pedicularia, also known as Yellow Vetch, is a perennial plant in the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally, the plant is used for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Dasistoma pedicularia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to create remedies for respiratory issues and fever.
